authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.23 Audit and accounting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public baseline for tribal gaming audit, accounting, documentation, and workpaper expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source qualification and evidence baseline setup

authoritative_sources.title: 25 CFR 543.24 Auditing revenue
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal regulation is the primary public rule for revenue-audit independence, reconciliation expectations, and operational-area review in tribal gaming.

authoritative_sources.title: NIGC Announces Record $43.9 Billion in FY 2024 Gross Gaming Revenues
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NIGC's official FY 2024 GGR release provides the public tribal gaming benchmark baseline and the agency's stated GGR definition based on independently audited statements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Benchmark framing and jurisdiction-specific source selection

authoritative_sources.title: Gaming Revenue Information (GRI)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada's official GRI page is an authoritative regulator source for monthly gaming revenue releases, market breakdowns, and public disclosure cadence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public revenue reporting and disclosure assembly

authoritative_sources.title: Nevada Gaming Abstract Report
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Nevada Gaming Abstract is an official regulator-issued analytical publication that provides operating-statistic framing and annual trend-comparison discipline for commercial gaming analysis.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Slots Version 9
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada slot MICS are authoritative for slot-system, meter, jackpot, payout, and drop-record expectations that materially change slot analytics reliability.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards Race and Sports Version 9
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada race and sports MICS are authoritative for wager, settlement, write-and-win, and account-wagering recordkeeping that constrains sports-betting analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Monthly Gross Revenue Report Instructions (DGE-101I)
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DGE instruction manual defines gross-revenue report lines, win and drop handling, adjustments, promotional-credit linkage, and attestation expectations for casino reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Monthly Internet Gross Revenue Report Instructions (DGE-105I)
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DGE instruction manual defines Internet gaming revenue categories, skin-level reporting, year-to-date tax logic, and promotional-credit treatment for online gaming analysis.
used_for: peer-to-peer versus other authorized game logic
internet gaming tax and promotional treatment

authoritative_sources.title: Monthly Sports Wagering Tax Return Instructions (DGE-107I)
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official DGE instruction manual defines sports wagering gross revenue, handle, cash-basis tax calculations, and mobile versus retail posture for sports-betting analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Commercial Gaming Revenue Tracker
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AGA's public revenue tracker is a recognized industry reference for monthly commercial gaming benchmarking and explicitly states that its insights are based on state regulatory reports.

workflow_stages.name: Source qualification and jurisdiction setup
workflow_stages.description: Identify the jurisdiction, property type, game vertical, channel mix, approved internal-control baseline, and reporting purpose that govern the analysis before any metric is selected.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric-definition and source-lineage mapping
workflow_stages.description: Determine which win, drop, handle, GGR, internet-gaming, sports-wagering, promotional, and patron-activity definitions apply and map each metric to named source systems and filing artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and exception review
workflow_stages.description: Compare gaming-system, accounting, revenue-audit, promotional, and regulator-facing records to identify matched metrics, unexplained variances, unsupported adjustments, and source-of-truth conflicts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis and disclosure package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble analytical briefs, dashboards, or disclosure-oriented summaries with explicit caveats, source lineage, refresh assumptions, and benchmark limitations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the work requires accounting signoff, tax certification, gaming-compliance interpretation, promotional-policy decisions, public-disclosure approval, or legal review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: slot meter recaps, jackpot logs, progressive reports, and slot performance extracts
table-games drop and win reports, fill and credit slips, rating summaries, and tournament records
sportsbook handle, revenue, ticket, settlement, void, and payout reports
internet gaming win files, skin-level reports, peer-to-peer versus other authorized game reports, and promotional-credit extracts
player-tracking, loyalty, and promotional liability records
cage, kiosk, voucher, and wagering-account support records
general-ledger balances, revenue-audit workpapers, and regulator filing workbooks
data warehouse, BI dashboard, and benchmark source packs
approved internal controls and source-precedence policy documents
systems_of_record: internet gaming and player-account management platforms
player loyalty, CRM, and promotional systems
general ledger and financial reporting systems
document repositories for filings, workpapers, and approved internal controls

allowed: Define gaming metrics and source hierarchies when the jurisdiction and reporting purpose are explicit.
Assemble evidence-backed analytical briefs, caveats, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk summaries.
Separate slot, table, sportsbook, internet gaming, and promotional record paths when the public baseline requires different evidence or definitions.
escalate: Jurisdiction, approved internal controls, or tenant source precedence are missing or conflict with the public baseline.
Regulator-facing reports, internal dashboards, and financial workpapers materially disagree on a publishable metric.
A requested claim requires accounting signoff, tax certification, compliance interpretation, promotional-policy authority, or disclosure approval.
refuse: Requests to certify regulator filings, approve revenue adjustments, or declare a benchmark or causal claim settled without evidence.
Requests to treat handle, drop, coin-in, hold, GGR, NGR, and promotional deductions as interchangeable without mapped definitions.
Requests that use the gaming industry label as cosmetic and skip jurisdiction, game-vertical, or control context.

domain_failure_modes: A portfolio trend is treated as comparable even though slot, table, sportsbook, and internet gaming metrics use different definitions and promotional treatments.
A dashboard treats one warehouse extract as the source of truth even when regulator filings, gaming systems, or revenue-audit workpapers disagree.
Public benchmark data is applied to property performance without matching jurisdiction, channel mix, reporting basis, or time horizon.
Unsupported causal, forecast, or disclosure language is published because a stakeholder wants a cleaner story than the evidence supports.
The lane drifts into accounting signoff, tax reporting, compliance interpretation, or promotional-policy authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Promotional deductions and net revenue treatment vary by jurisdiction and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Cross-property or cross-jurisdiction comparisons can overstate or understate revenue quality if promotional treatment is assumed rather than retrieved.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require jurisdiction-specific filing rules, tenant metric definitions, and approved source precedence before selecting GGR, NGR, or adjusted revenue views.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Land-based, mobile wagering, and internet gaming channels often use different system owners and refresh windows.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Trend packages can look complete even when one channel is delayed or governed by a different filing basis.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require channel-level freshness checks and source timestamps before blending outputs into one trend conclusion.

notes: The research set covers regulator rules, public reporting instructions, monthly and annual revenue disclosures, and recognized benchmark framing tied directly to gaming analytics.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because every identified ambiguity has an explicit runtime retrieval or escalation rule.
